ogTitle: Magitup vs Mahogany Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Magitup and Mahogany Creek.
heading: Magitup vs Mahogany Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Magitup, Western Australia and Mahogany Creek, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mahogany Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Magitup (30/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Magitup or Mahogany Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Magitup has a flood risk score of 20/100, while Mahogany Creek scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Magitup scores 22/100 for bushfire risk. Mahogany Creek scores 26/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Magitup or Mahogany Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mahogany Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.03% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
